architecture_notes: SyncEngine stays the facade: it drives the single recursive, parallel pre-order walk, builds the entry union, applies excludes, assigns and conforms peer roles, threads the dry-run flag, enqueues copies into the copy queue, and enforces the listing-failure subtree invariants.

It delegates the two heaviest, cleanly separable concerns: DecisionRules is the pure per-path resolver (classification plus the file, directory, and type-conflict matrix), and Displacement performs the inline BAK rename the walk calls when a decision requires moving an entry aside.

children.name: DecisionRules
children.responsibility: Given each peer's live state, snapshot row, and role at one path, classify it and resolve the contributing peers (with canon override) into a single outcome -- which file version wins, whether a directory is created or displaced, and how file/directory type conflicts are settled.

children.name: Displacement
children.responsibility: Perform one inline displacement to BAK: create the per-parent .kitchensync/BAK/<timestamp>/ directory and rename the entry into it as a single subtree move, log and leave the entry in place on rename failure, and suppress the move under dry-run.
